steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage states that most light from distant galaxies is infrared, then makes a claim about the JWST. Because the JWST is an infrared telescope, this fact about galaxy light directly explains its advantage — the second clause is a consequence of the first. The blank needs a transition that signals "therefore" or "as a result."

steps.title: Eliminate wrong transitions and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — consequently,: signals a direct result or logical consequence — exactly right, because the JWST's ability to detect infrared light follows from the fact that most distant-galaxy light is infrared. ✓
  • Choice B — in contrast,: signals opposition between two ideas. The two clauses are not opposing — they reinforce each other. ✗
  • Choice C — additionally,: signals a separate, added point. The JWST clause is not an extra fact; it is the result of the preceding fact. ✗
  • Choice D — actually,: signals a surprising correction or reversal. The JWST's capability is the expected outcome, not a surprising contradiction. ✗
That matches Choice A — consequently,.
